mission: Check conformance calculations use real formulas, not pre-computed scores

mathematical_components: consumed - actual token consumption from trace
produced - actual token production from trace
missing - actual token deficit from trace

calculate_conformance/2.formula: let consumed_ratio = consumed as f64 / produced as f64; let produced_ratio = produced as f64 / (produced + remaining) as f64; let score = 0.5 * consumed_ratio.min(1.0) + 0.5 * produced_ratio;
calculate_conformance/2.complexity: HIGH - mathematical operations with floating point

token_replay/2.formula: let pr = if produced > 0 { consumed as f64 / produced as f64 } else { 1.0 }; let mr = if consumed + missing > 0 { 1.0 - missing as f64 / (consumed + missing) as f64 } else { 1.0 }; score = 0.5 * pr.min(1.0) + 0.5 * mr;

alignment_score/2.notes: No alignment_score function found in codebase

formula_inconsistency.description: Two different formulas implemented across codebase:

anti_rounding_modifications.description: Artificial score manipulation in lib.rs implementation
anti_rounding_modifications.impact: MEDIUM - Prevents perfect scores, may hide true conformance

no_hardcoded_shortcuts.finding: All implementations calculate scores from actual token counts
no_hardcoded_shortcuts.evidence: No constant score returns found, all use mathematical operations

calculate_conformance/2.VERDICT: PASS - Uses real formula with mathematical operations

token_replay/2.VERDICT: PASS - Uses real formula with token counting

alignment_score/2.VERDICT: FAIL - Function not found in codebase

test_cases.name: Perfect Conformance
test_cases.verdict: PASS - Score calculated from actual counts

test_cases.name: No Tokens
test_cases.verdict: PASS - Edge case handled by formula

recommendations.action: Standardize formula across all implementations - choose either 'missing' or 'remaining' approach
Remove artificial score modifications to get true conformance measures
Implement missing alignment score function if needed for complete conformance checking

conclusion.summary: Conformance formulas use real calculations from token counts, but formula inconsistency between implementations needs to be resolved
conclusion.confidence: HIGH - Verified through code analysis and testing
